title: dalbergia lanceolaria subsp. paniculata
intro: Dalbergia lanceolaria subsp. paniculata, commonly known as the 'Brazilian rosewood', is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Dalbergia lanceolaria subsp. paniculata include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it is often used in traditional medicine, particularly in Brazil, where it is considered a valuable resource for its medicinal and economic benefits.
